title: Accountability for Terrorist Perpetrators of October 7th Act
short_title: Accountability for Terrorist Perpetrators of October 7th Act
plain_summary: This law makes the President put sanctions on a terrorist group called the Popular Resistance Committees within ninety days. Sanctions mean these groups cannot do business with America and their members cannot travel to the United States. The law also makes the Secretary of State write a report about whether other terrorist groups should get the same punishment. This is all because of terrorist attacks that happened on October 7th.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provi

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Accountability for Terrorist Perpetrators of October 7th Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Imposition of Terrorism Sanctions with Respect to the Popular Resistance Committees
sections.text: Congress has learned several important facts. The Popular Resistance Committees (called the PRC) was started in two thousand during a violent uprising. Since then, it has regularly attacked Israelis, Americans, and Palestinians. The group includes former members from Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine. The United States has already labeled all three of these groups as foreign terrorist organizations. The PRC attacked a United States diplomatic convoy in two thousand three. This attack hurt one diplomat and killed three security guards from the United States. The PRC also murdered a pregnant Israeli woman and her four daughters in two thousand four. The daughters were eleven, nine, seven, and two years old. The group has also killed Palestinians, including a former Palestinian Authority Gaza security chief. By two thousand eleven, the PRC had become the third largest terror group in the Gaza strip. Only Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad were larger. On October seventh, two thousand twenty three, Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and the Popular Resistance Committees terrorists started a massive war against Israel without warning. They attacked by air, land, and sea. They fired thousands of rockets. This resulted in the deaths of one thousand two hundred innocent Israelis, Americans, and others. They kidnapped two hundred fifty hostages. They also committed widespread torture and sexual violence. On October seventh, two thousand twenty three, the PRC released a statement claiming they helped with the terrorist attack against Israel alongside Hamas and their partners. They said they kidnapped Israeli military soldiers. They posted photos of items they took from soldiers they killed and captured as proof. On October eighth, two thousand twenty three, the PRC released another statement. They claimed they sent suicide drones during the attack on Israel. Starting ninety days after this law is passed, the President must impose the punishments described later in this section. These punishments apply to the Popular Resistance Committees. They also apply to any foreign person who is an official, agent, or partner of the Popular Resistance Committees. They apply to any foreign person who is owned or controlled by the Popular Resistance Committees or their officials, agents, or partners. They also apply to any current or future armed organization that the President decides is operating under the Popular Resistance Committees or any other person described in this section. The punishments are as follows. The President must use all powers granted under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to block and prohibit all transactions involving property and interests in property of these persons. This applies if such property and interests are in the United States, come into the United States, or are controlled by a United States person. Any foreign person described in this section cannot enter the United States. The Secretary of State or the Secretary of Homeland Security (or someone they designate) will determine this. If they know or have reason to believe someone is described in this section, that person cannot be admitted to the United States. They cannot get a visa or other documentation to enter the United States. They cannot get any other benefit under the Immigration and Nationality Act. The issuing consular officer, the Secretary of State, or the Secretary of Homeland Security (or someone they designate) must cancel any visa or other entry documentation given to these people. This happens regardless of when the visa or other documentation was issued. The cancellation takes effect immediately and automatically cancels any other valid visa or entry documentation the person has. There are two exceptions to the travel bans. The travel ban does not apply if letting the person into the United States is necessary to comply with the Agreement regarding the Headquarters of the United Nations. This agreement was signed at Lake Success on June twenty sixth, nineteen forty seven, and went into force on November twenty first, nineteen forty seven. The travel ban also does not apply to other applicable international obligations. The punishments also do not apply to any authorized intelligence, law enforcement, or national security activities of the United States. The penalties provided in the International Emergency Economic Powers Act will apply to anyone who violates, tries to violate, plans to violate, or causes a violation of this section. This includes violations of regulations made to carry out this section or the sanctions imposed under this section. The same penalties that apply to unlawful acts under that Act will apply here. The President may use all authorities provided under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to carry out this section. The President may waive the application of sanctions or restrictions imposed on a foreign person under this section. The waiver can be for one or more periods of not more than one hundred eighty days each. The President must certify to the appropriate congressional committees not later than fifteen days before the waiver takes effect that the waiver is vital to the national security interests of the United States. Sanctions under this section may be ended with respect to any foreign person if the President certifies to the appropriate congressional committees one of two things. First, that the foreign person is no longer engaging in acts of terrorism as defined in Executive Order Number thirteen two two four. This order relates to blocking property and prohibiting transactions with persons who commit, threaten to commit, or support terrorism. Second, if the foreign person is an organization, that the organization has disbanded.

sections.title: Section 3  Report on the Designation of the Lion's Den and the Popular Resistance Committees as Specially Designated Global Terrorists
sections.text: Congress has found the following facts. Lion's Den was founded in two thousand twenty two. Since then, it has carried out many terror attacks including over sixty one shooting attacks against Israeli soldiers and civilians. The group includes former members from Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad, and the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine. All of these groups are designated foreign terrorist organizations. Lion's Den receives money from designated foreign terrorist organizations Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad. Not later than ninety days after this law is passed, the Secretary of State must submit a detailed report to the appropriate committees of Congress. The report must say whether Lion's Den meets the criteria for designation as a Specially Designated Global Terrorist under Executive Order Number thirteen two two four. This order relates to blocking property and prohibiting transactions with persons who commit, threaten to commit, or support terrorism. The report must also say whether Lion's Den meets the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization as set forth in the Immigration and Nationality Act. The report must say whether the Popular Resistance Committees meet the criteria as a Specially Designated Global Terrorist under Executive Order Number thirteen two two four. If the Secretary decides that any of these entities do not meet the criteria, the report must include a detailed explanation of which criteria have not been met. The report must be submitted in unclassified form, but may include a classified section. Not later than one year after this law is passed, and every two years after that, the Secretary of State must submit a report to the appropriate committees of Congress. The report must identify each new organization operating under the Popular Resistance Committees or any successor of the Popular Resistance Committees. The report must include a determination whether each such organization meets the criteria for designation as a Specially Designated Global Terrorist under Executive Order Number thirteen two two four. The report must also say whether they meet the criteria for designation as a foreign terrorist organization under the Immigration and Nationality Act. The report must say whether they meet the criteria for the imposition of sanctions under section two of this Act. If the Secretary decides that any of the organizations do not meet the criteria, the report must include a detailed explanation of which criteria have not been met.

sections.title: Section 4  Appropriate Committees of Congress Defined
sections.text: In this law, the term appropriate committees of Congress means the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives.
